steps.title: Find the constant c using the given information about function p
steps.content:
Function $p$ is linear with slope 8, so its equation is $p(x) = 8x + b$ for some y-intercept $b$.

From $p(5) = 42$:
$$8(5) + b = 42$$
$$40 + b = 42$$
$$b = 2$$

Therefore, $p(x) = 8x + 2$.

From $p(c) = -6$:
$$8c + 2 = -6$$
$$8c = -8$$
$$c = -1$$

steps.title: Find the slope of t using two known points
steps.content:
Now that we know $c = -1$, function $t$ passes through two points:
  • $(c, t(c)) = (-1, -7)$
  • $(6, t(6)) = (6, 56)$

Using the slope formula:
$$m_t = \frac{56 - (-7)}{6 - (-1)} = \frac{63}{7} = 9$$

That matches choice D — D is correct.
